title: pteridium aquilinum subsp. feei
intro: Pteridium aquilinum subsp. feei, also known as the bracken fern, is a subspecies of the bracken fern plant (Pteridium aquilinum) that is native to Asia and Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The bracken fern is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting kidney and liver function. In herbal preparations, the bracken fern is typically dried and used as a tea or infused in hot water to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
